title: Cloud Run Functions: Qwik Start
aim: To create Cloud Run Functions that respond to HTTP calls, Cloud Storage events, and Cloud Audit Logs, and explore advanced settings like revisions, minimum instances, and concurrency.

steps.title: Enable the Required APIs
steps.description: Go to APIs & Services > Enable APIs. Enable: Cloud Run, Cloud Functions, Cloud Build, Artifact Registry, Eventarc APIs.

steps.title: Create an HTTP Function
steps.description: Create directory, write main.py and requirements.txt, then deploy using gcloud functions deploy.

steps.title: Create a Cloud Storage Function
steps.description: Create a GCS bucket, write function code, and deploy with a storage trigger (google.cloud.storage.object.v1.finalized).

steps.title: Deploy Multiple Revisions
steps.description: Redeploy the function with an environment variable to create a new revision. Check traffic split in Console.

steps.title: Configure Scalability
steps.description: Set minimum instances and concurrency using 'gcloud run services update'.

commands.purpose: Set minimum instances to reduce cold starts

viva.question: What are Cloud Run Functions?
viva.answer: They are a serverless execution environment for building and connecting cloud services.

viva.question: What is the benefit of Gen2 functions?
viva.answer: Gen2 functions are built on Cloud Run and Eventarc, offering longer timeouts, larger instance sizes, and better concurrency.

notes: Cloud Run Functions (Gen2) are powered by Cloud Run, supporting up to 1000 concurrent requests, 60-min HTTP timeout, and up to 16GB RAM.
